summary.what: Add doctor detection and --fix for outdated post-rewrite hooks
summary.why: Installed hooks never picked up generator improvements — hooks install skips when a section is present, so the dash/POSIX fix couldn't reach existing repos and nothing surfaced the staleness; this is the upgrade gap discovered while shipping the relink warning
summary.how: doctor compares the installed post-rewrite section to the current generator output and warns on drift; doctor --fix regenerates via new setup helpers SectionUpToDate + ReplaceTimbersSection (remove-then-append, preserving non-timbers content)

notes: Direct content comparison over a stamped version number (the bead floated version/hash markers): comparing the installed section against postRewriteTimbersSection() keeps a single source of truth, nothing to bump or go stale. Scoped to post-rewrite only — pre/post-commit hooks are thin shims to the binary, so only post-rewrite carries self-contained logic that can rot. Reused existing remove/append section machinery.
